project.name: spring-rest-sakila
project.description: A Spring Boot-based REST API service that provides CRUD and complex query access to the MySQL Sakila sample database, which models a DVD rental store company. The service exposes endpoints for managing films, actors, customers, rentals, payments, staff, stores, and locations, complete with role-based access control, JWT authentication, and comprehensive API documentation via OpenAPI/Swagger and Postman.
project.architecture_summary: The application follows a layered architecture organized into distinct domain modules (films, customers, rentals, payments, staff, stores, locations, reports, and authentication). Each domain contains DTOs, entities, repositories, services, and controllers. The architecture employs REST representational models with HAL/HATEOAS support for hypermedia-driven API responses. Cross-cutting concerns are handled through configuration classes, filters, and converters. Data access uses Spring Data JPA repositories enhanced with custom QueryDSL and Blaze JPA queries for complex filtering. MapStruct is used for entity-to-DTO mapping, and Spring Security provides JWT-based authentication with role-based authorization.

Retrieves comprehensive details for a specific store, accessible only to users with ROLE_MANAGE permission.

@RequiredArgsConstructor suggests dependency injection of service beans via constructor

classes.description: A MapStruct mapper for converting between InventoryEntity and InventoryDto, with selective field mapping to exclude related entities.

methods.description: Converts an InventoryEntity to an InventoryDto, explicitly ignoring the filmByFilmId and storeByStoreId relationship fields to prevent deep object mapping.

notable_aspects: Uses @Mapping annotations to explicitly ignore relationship fields (filmByFilmId, storeByStoreId), preventing unnecessary loading of related entities and avoiding potential circular references

classes.description: A MapStruct mapper for converting between StoreDto and StoreEntity, with automatic timestamp generation and selective relationship mapping.

methods.description: Converts a StoreDto to a StoreEntity, generating the current timestamp for lastUpdate, ignoring the storeId auto-generation, and excluding relationship fields (staffByManagerStaffId, addressByAddressId).

notable_aspects: Uses expression-based mapping to dynamically set lastUpdate to LocalDateTime.now() during conversion, ensuring fresh timestamps on entity creation
Selectively ignores relationship fields and the primary key to allow database-level handling of auto-increment and foreign key relationships
